---
title: "Urban Land-Use Efficiency"
subtitle: "A Stable Metric for SDG 11.3.1 and Its Economic and Structural Drivers"
author: "Ömer Furkan Çoban"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: [litera, 05_paper/theme.scss]
    embed-resources: true
    toc: false
    anchor-sections: false
    link-external-newwindow: true
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Development Economics seminar · SoSe 2026 · Carl von Ossietzky University of
Oldenburg (Applied Economics and Data Science) · Lecturer: Prof. Dr. Jürgen Bitzer.
:::

The official SDG 11.3.1 indicator (LCRPGR) is unstable where population growth is
near zero. This project proposes the **Built-up per Capita Rate (BpCR)**, a stable
log-based metric, builds a harmonised global panel of 193 UN member states
(1985–2020) from GHSL settlement data under the Degree of Urbanisation, and
estimates the economic and structural drivers of per-capita urban land use with
two-way fixed-effects and dynamic-panel GMM.

## Outputs

#### Paper
- [Read online (HTML)](05_paper/paper.html)
- [Download PDF](05_paper/paper.pdf)

#### Supplementary — Sub-national case study: Germany (Kreis level)
- [Read online (HTML)](05_paper/supplementary.html)
- [Download PDF](05_paper/supplementary.pdf)

#### Presentation
- [Open slides (HTML)](04_presentation/presentation.html)
- [Download PDF](04_presentation/presentation.pdf)

## Reproducibility

The full chain (data → panel → figures → render) is orchestrated by
`run_pipeline.R`. The repository ships the committed processed panels, so a clean
checkout renders the paper, supplementary, and presentation without credentials;
`--gee` rebuilds the raw inputs from Google Earth Engine and INKAR.
